name: Solar Conduction Dryer
description: The Solar Conduction Dryer (SCD) is a solar powered food dehydrator that uses all three types of heat transfer modes (conduction, convection and radiation) to dry food.

taxonomy.sub_category: Solar Conduction Dryer Updated on August 21, 2024 · Created on June 8, 2017 Solar Conduction Dryer The Solar Conduction Dryer (SCD) is a solar powered food dehydrator that uses all three types of heat transfer modes (conduction, convection and radiation) to dry food. Developed By Science for Society Tested By International Food Laboratories Content Partners Unknown Info Tags Snapshot Manufacturing & Delivery Performance & Use Research & Standards Feedback Product Description The Solar Conduction Dryer (SCD) is a solar powered food dehydrator, that uses all three types of heat transfer modes; conduction, convection and radiation. The product’s business model aims to integrate key stakeholders to create an ecosystem where the SCD creates a business opportunity for dehydrated food commercialization. A 10-12 kg capacity SCD has a $542.76 price in India (converted from Indian Ruppes 6/23/2017) and the cost outside India is $640. For a 100 kg capacity SCD the price in India is $4342.13 and outside India $5,120. These prices do not include transportation and duties. Interview with representative. Made to order, loading capacity is 10-12 kg but can be customized per customer need. Target SDGs SDG 2: Zero Hunger SDG 1: No Poverty Market Suggested Retail Price $5,120.00 Target Users (Target Impact Group) Household, Community, Small and Medium-sized Enterprises Distributors / Implementing Organizations The product is produced in India and distributors are being created in Kenya, also it is distributed through some NGO's and implementation agencies. Competitive Landscape Direct competitors include Kinosol Orenda Food Dehydrator , SunWorks Solar Food Dryer , and GoSol Sol4 Oven . Regions East Africa, South Asia Countries India, Kenya, Sri Lanka, Vietnam Manufacturing/Building Method Made to order, loading capacity is 10-12 kg but can be customized per customer need. Intellectural Property Type Patent User Provision Model Directly from manufacturer; S4S technologies. Distributions to Date Status Around 650 solar conduction dryers are operating in India and Kenya, impacting 3,000 lives. More than 1,200 farmer cooperatives across 8 countries are using it. Drying capacity (lbs/m³) 39.4 Drying duration (hr) 12 hours of solar Load capacity (m³) 0.67 Operating temperature range (°C) 39–51 °C Temperature control No Number of trays 4 Materials of construction Trays are coated with black color special food grade coating Design Specifications The SCD has four drying chambers constructed from hollow sections of stainless steel. Each chamber has a drying tray which is covered with transparent plastic. The trays are coated with black color special food grade coating, where the products to be dried are placed. A low height air vent to create air current is provided at the middle of the dryer along horizontal direction which also separates the drying chambers in two parts. Product Schematics Technical Support The manufacturer offers technical support as well as training through a partnership with NGO's and implementation agencies. Replacement Components Unknown Lifecycle Unknown Manufacturer Specified Performance Parameters Can be easily assembled by two people locally. Has a capacity of 12 kg/dryer. Able to maintain 45% more nutrients. Vetted Performance Status Dehydrated products retain 80%-90% of key nutrients. It is said to increase in shelf life of products from 6 -12 months and to reduce 40% in post harvest losses. It meets international standards in terms of microbial count and aflatoxin level. The product is said to save 20 tons of CO2 throughout its lifetime. Interview with representative Safety Risk of burns from handling hot trays. Complementary Technical Systems The SCD designer identifies food processing companies which are interested in buying dehydrated products, and makes field trials to create samples for those companies so that a business contract can be made. The designer also works closely with NGO/implementation agencies to train farmers on the dryer's usage, processing and packaging. Academic Research and References Borah. A, Hazarika. K, Khayer S.M. 2015. Drying kinetics of whole and sliced turmeric rhizomes (Curcuma longa L.) in a solar conduction dryer . Information Processsing Agriculture. 2(2):85-92. Compliance with regulations The manufacturer has indicated compliance with international standards regarding microbial count and aflatoxin level. Materials such as stainless steel and black coating paint are special for food grade applications.
